dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The salt spray kissed his face, a cold, insistent reminder. His head throbbed, a dull, persistent ache.

dialogue.speaker: PROTAGONIST
dialogue.line: My head... it's splitting. Where am I? What happened?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: His eyes fluttered open to an unfamiliar stretch of sand, an endless grey sky.

dialogue.speaker: PROTAGONIST
dialogue.line: My name... it's gone. Everything is gone.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: WAVES (GENTLE LAPPING)

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: HEARTBEAT (THUMP-THUMP, FASTER)

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Panic, cold and sharp, began to claw at his throat. He pushed himself up, limbs aching. In the distance, through sparse trees, a silhouette.

dialogue.speaker: PROTAGONIST
dialogue.line: A house? On an island like this?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: It was squat, decaying, its windows dark like vacant eyes. A strange, unsettling presence.

dialogue.speaker: PROTAGONIST
dialogue.line: Maybe someone there can help me.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The walk was long, the ground uneven. The closer he got, the stronger the putrid smell became.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: WIND (WHISTLING FAINTLY)

dialogue.speaker: PROTAGONIST
dialogue.line: What is that smell? Rot... and something else.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The house loomed, larger and more dilapidated. Paint peeled, revealing dark, rotting wood. A broken porch swing creaked.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: He hesitated at the heavy, scarred front door. No knocker. He pushed.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: DOOR (CREAKING LOUDLY)

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The door groaned open with a sound like a dying animal. The air inside was thick, stagnant, heavy with decay. Dust motes danced in slivers of light.

dialogue.speaker: PROTAGONIST
dialogue.line: Hello? Is anyone here?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: His voice sounded small, swallowed by oppressive silence. He stepped further in, eyes adjusting. The living room was a chaotic mess of old, stained furniture.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: DRIP (DRIP-DRIP, STEADY, FROM SOMEWHERE DEEP INSIDE)

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: FOOTSTEPS (SOFT, CAUTIOUS)

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: A sound from deeper within. A low, guttural cough.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: COUGH (HARSH, WET)

dialogue.speaker: PROTAGONIST
dialogue.line: Someone is here.

dialogue.speaker: OLD MAN
dialogue.line: Well, well, look what the tide dragged in.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: An old man emerged from shadows, face a roadmap of wrinkles, eyes sharp. He held a rusty cleaver.

dialogue.speaker: OLD MAN
dialogue.line: Lost your way, boy? Or just... lost?

dialogue.speaker: PROTAGONIST
dialogue.line: I... I don't know. I woke up on the beach. I can't remember anything.

dialogue.speaker: OLD MAN
dialogue.line: Amnesia, eh? A convenient affliction.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: CLEAVER (SCRAPE, GENTLE, AGAINST A WOODEN BLOCK)

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The old man grinned, a wide, unsettling smile that didn't reach his eyes.

dialogue.speaker: OLD MAN
dialogue.line: Don't worry, boy. You're safe now. This is a good place for lost things.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: From behind him, a woman appeared. Face blank, eyes vacant. She carried a bucket, dripping with something dark.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: DRIP (DRIP-DRIP, FASTER NOW)

dialogue.speaker: PROTAGONIST
dialogue.line: What's in that bucket? And her eyes...

dialogue.speaker: OLD MAN
dialogue.line: My wife. She's a bit shy. But she'll take good care of you. We all will.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The woman didn't speak, just stared, her gaze unsettlingly empty.

dialogue.speaker: OLD MAN
dialogue.line: We've been expecting you.

dialogue.speaker: PROTAGONIST
dialogue.line: Expecting me? This is wrong. All of it.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: HEARTBEAT (LOUD, POUNDING)

dialogue.speaker: OLD MAN
dialogue.line: Come, boy. You must be hungry. We have plenty of... food.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: He gestured towards a dark hallway. The woman moved to block the front door.

dialogue.speaker: PROTAGONIST
dialogue.line: I need to get out of here. Now.

summary: A man wakes up on a desolate beach with no memory, only to discover a dilapidated house inhabited by a strange, unsettling old couple who seem to have been expecting him.
